code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test24-JAN-2025Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, mild ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no hematuria or leukocyturia. These findings are consistent with possible renal dysfunction, such as diabetic nephropathy or another glomerular pathology. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
